_comment: contract-desk reviews inbound brand contracts, assembles plain-language drafts, and (Phase 3) extracts obligations from a signed contract and debriefs a closed deal. Legal information only, never legal advice. Gated behind contract_management. Every output carries the RESEARCH NOTES boundary, human_review_required, and passes govern-artifact. Reuses usage-rights-check and exclusivity-check as the extraction/conflict core. The obligation date math runs offline in tools/obligations.py (no tokens); this spoke never signs, sends, or writes the register itself.
description: Contract review, drafting, version tracing, obligations, and deal debrief for brand partnerships.

steps.condition: action is review or full, and triage is not a clear walk-away RED; requires contract_redline

steps.input_from: versions[] from the contract record (contract_id) or supplied versions
steps.condition: action is trace or full, and two or more versions with resolvable text exist; requires contract_redline
action is legal_check or full; requires legal_requirement_checks

steps.input_from: flagged findings from steps 1 to 4, including amendment-trace conflicts and watch_items
steps.condition: action is escalate or full, and any flags, RED/YELLOW items, conflicts, or watch_items exist
action is draft; requires contract_drafting; independent of the review chain
action is obligations; requires contract_obligations and a signed contract. Emits obligation rows; tools/obligations.py computes the dated register offline (no tokens).

note: Master gate: contract_management. Intake reuses the uploaded_file / google_drive document connectors (no new connector). Phase 3 adds obligation-extract (action obligations; pulls duties from a SIGNED contract into rows for tools/obligations.py, which computes send-by dates, weekend/US-holiday roll-back, and urgency bands offline and writes the gitignored register only when contract_obligations is on) and deal-debrief (action debrief; proposal-only playbook memory after a deal closes, never writes the playbook). Obligations feed the existing content-calendar / production-task / deal-resourcing join points via the import_obligations MCP handoff, never a parallel calendar. This spoke never signs, sends, or writes personal records; escalation-brief and contract-draft are draft-only.
